free_text: F.Fabiani.
Fluctuations in total neutron cross-sections of Na,
Al, Si, P, S, in the energy interval from 5.0 to
8.5 MeV
5.5 MeV Van de Graaff of the University
of Padua.
Deuterons incident on a deuterium gas cell
through a 1.5 micro-m thick nickel window.
.Neutron energy spread 30 to 40 keV taking into
account:
1.Intrinsic energy spread of deuteron beam.
2.Straggling in Ni window of gas target.
3.Energy loss in target.
4.Angular spread of deuteron beam by Coulomb
scattering in the window.
5.Finite angle of detector.
.Chemically pure natural elements in the form of
small cylinders 30 mm in diameter and of length
chosen to give 0.5 to 0.7 transmission. Sodium
and white phosphorous samples contained in light
cans. Identical dummy cans used for background
subtraction. Samples placed 30 cm from source.
Time-of-flight.
Long counter used as flux monitor in
conjunction with beam current integration.
Cross-sections are mean values of several
measurements.
From NEUDADA, data originally from private
communication, November 1966.
.Corrections for dead time of the counter chain
(1 to 2%) and for spurious neutrons not
resolved by the TOF system (about 3%). The
latter correction found by shielding the detector
with a 1 m iron bar.
.Statistical errors only are given, other errors
estimated to be small.
.Neutron energy uncertainty of 5 keV due to the
uncertainties in beam energy calibration and in
nickel foil thickness.
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
